Extract variable labels from a data.table

Source: R/extractLabels.R
extractLabels.Rd

This function retrieves the "label" attribute from a set of columns in a data.table and returns them in a two-column data.table.

Usage

extractLabels(dt, cols)

Arguments

dt

A data.table containing labelled variables.

cols

A character vector of column names from which to extract labels.

Value

A data.table with two columns:

variable

Column name.

label

Value of the "label" attribute for the column (may be NULL).

Details

The function assumes that labels are stored as attributes named "label" If a column does not have a "label" attribute, NA is returned.

Examples

library(data.table)

dt <- data.table(
  age = c(20, 30),
  sex = c(1, 2)
)
attr(dt$age, "label") <- "Age of respondent"
attr(dt$sex, "label") <- "Biological sex"

extractLabels(dt, c("age", "sex"))
#>    variable             label
#>      <char>            <char>
#> 1:      age Age of respondent
#> 2:      sex    Biological sex
